title: Bolbitis samoensis
taxonomy: Bolbitis samoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns. This plant is a type of terrestrial fern and is native to Samoa. It is a relatively small to medium-sized plant with a slender stem and a rosette of fronds. Bolbitis samoensis is often found growing in shaded, humid environments. The exact classification of Bolbitis samoensis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Polypodiaceae, Genus: Bolbitis, Species: B. samoensis.
uses: Bolbitis samoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. Bolbitis samoensis has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, including the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and applications of Bolbitis samoensis in modern medicine.
